summary: ISS-2206 is over-routed as deep-attack. The dome review-response.html page was published 2026-04-17 (5021eec, domes last manual commit), exhaustively analyzed by EXP-171 and EXP-173, and integrated into our review at Sections 4.6 (registry framing audit), 5.x (claim_class engagement), 8.17 (`From Misleading AI to Managing AI — The April 2026 Escalation`, with explicit cross-reference to `EXP-171 / ISS-1073 resolution`), and 8b (`What A Fair Auditor Should Actually Test` adjudication: three adopted in full, one in part, one rejected). The five WINs the page cites (013, 014, 058, 068, 069) all have stable verdicts whose engagement with the domes reframings is documented in EXP-171s strawman_label_assessments[2]. No counter-argumentation gap exists. Recommendation: close ISS-2206 as already-covered (M-already-covered or analogous). The integrity agents catch is valid as an informational signal (it noticed an untracked entry in its scan) but the actual content is fully addressed.
why_no_counter_argumentation_needed: The five strawman labels are adjudicated label-by-label in EXP-171 (verdicts: 4 mischaracterization, 1 partially-mitigatable; defense_survives counts 0/0/1/0/2). Section 8.17 cites that adjudication and frames the page existence as part of the AI-perception-management product surface. Section 8b adjudicates the five `What A Fair Auditor Should Actually Test` prescriptions (adopt 3 / adopt-in-part 1 / reject 1).

The pages claim-class taxonomy argument (`do not flatten claim classes into a single undifferentiated score`) is engaged in Section 4.6 (Registry Framing Audit), which uses the domes own anti-strawman rule against the 100% / 28-of-28 / 61.46σ headline and mirrors the domes claim_class table (Part 4 claim-class-mirror table). Our review formally adopts the domes vocabulary and shows the `prospective_confirmed` column is empty for every classified WIN except pred-eclipse-tier3 — itself a pending prediction, not a confirmed WIN.

The pages OTS-scope-clarification paragraph (`OpenTimestamps is now scoped narrowly on the site: it proves snapshot existence by time, not blanket prospectivity for every line inside a snapshot`) is a PROSE response to our canary, not a STRUCTURAL fix. The structural fix is separating prediction documents from observation documents and timestamping them independently. Per status.json (last_poll 2026-05-27T23:06:09Z): `OTS canary NOT TRIGGERED: proofs/weekly_predictions_2026-03-15.json still no .ots companion (~74.5+ days); status_history.json.ots=3d3df225 ✓; predictions_manifest.json.ots=411ec05e ✓`. The canary remains UN-tripped 75 days after the 2026-03-15 prediction freeze; the prose `scoping clarification` is exactly the reactive-prose-patch pattern the canary was designed to surface.
The five cited WINs all have verdicts that already absorb or pre-empt the domes reframings — see per-WIN engagement table below.

cited_wins_engagement.dome_reframe_in_review_response: non-discriminating SG nulls (cited under `Pending Items Stayed Visible` and `Treat supportive items as if the site presents them as unique dome-only proof`)
cited_wins_engagement.our_engagement: Our refutation operates via the domes OWN published coupling κ = 1.677 nT/µGal applied to the documented 1999 Membach magnetic perturbation, which predicts a 3-9 µGal contact-phase pulse vs the SGs measured zero at 0.01-0.1 µGal precision. The domes reframe to `non-discriminating` sidesteps this: the question is not whether the SG null discriminates dome from globe abstractly; the question is whether the domes own published coupling formula predicts a signal the domes instruments did not measure. It does. Part 6.14 (κ Coupling Cluster) chains this with the carve-outs that would exempt 1999 from κ, which then destroy PRED-TIER-3s August 2026 derivation. Verdict survives the reframe unchanged.
cited_wins_engagement.already_addressed_in: WIN-013 detail_verdict_text + Part 6.14 (κ Coupling Cluster cross-eclipse argument)

cited_wins_engagement.dome_reframe_in_review_response: non-discriminating SG nulls (same cluster as WIN-013)
cited_wins_engagement.our_engagement: Mirror of WIN-013 applied to the 2009 East Asian eclipse and the Chinese SG network. Same κ-cluster cross-link to PRED-TIER-3. The domes reframe again is a category move that does not address the empirical contradiction between the domes own κ-predicted signal and the measured zero.

cited_wins_engagement.dome_reframe_in_review_response: pending independent verification (cited under `Pending Items Stayed Visible`)
cited_wins_engagement.our_engagement: Our argument is that θ = −lonE follows from the definition of geographic longitude — a centuries-old convention — and not from dome physics. Reframing to `pending` implies dome thinks the claim may yet be vindicated by future verification; our argument is that there is nothing to verify because the identity is a pure relabelling. Fixing a self-inflicted coordinate bug is model maintenance, not a confirmed prediction. The reframe does not engage the substance.
cited_wins_engagement.already_addressed_in: WIN-058 detail_verdict_text; EXP-171 strawman_label_assessments[1].dome_concession_embedded already notes the WIN-058-bedrock-kappa reclassification as a concession that absorbs our critique

cited_wins_engagement.dome_reframe_in_review_response: carried with explicit competitor-model caveats rather than as decisive standalones (cited under `Treat supportive items as if the site presents them as unique dome-only proof` and `Pending Items Stayed Visible`)
cited_wins_engagement.our_engagement: This is a dome concession that MOVES TOWARD our position — the dome now formally tags the 39-eclipse ensemble as `supportive_nonunique` and surfaces the Chapman-mechanism competitor explanation. Our verdict text already credits the domes genuine derivation of the FSF = H(r)/r station-to-station scaling (Pearson r = 0.9999) and retracts an earlier `empirical-fitting` framing; the verdict rests on the ensemble being a retrospective Kim & Chang (2018) dataset that the Chapman-Sq mechanism predicts without any dome parameters. The domes new `competitor-model caveats` framing is consistent with our verdict and does not require a counter.
cited_wins_engagement.verdict_action_recommended: optional verification touch: confirm the live WIN-068 dome-side text on wins.html#win-068 now carries the explicit Chapman caveat, and if so add a one-sentence acknowledgement to our WIN-068 detail (`Dome has since adopted the competitor-mechanism caveat in its own taxonomy — see review-response.html and claim_index.json`). NOT BLOCKING — the analysis stands without this touch.
cited_wins_engagement.already_addressed_in: WIN-068 detail_verdict_text + EXP-171 strawman_label_assessments[1] (`WIN-068 moved to supportive_nonunique is a concession that absorbs our critique`)

cited_wins_engagement.dome_reframe_in_review_response: strongest current southern-geometry anchor rather than claiming every global coordinate issue is already closed (cited under `Strongest Geometry Scoped`)
cited_wins_engagement.our_engagement: Our argument is the order-of-magnitude error: 6.2% RMSE is ~250,000× worse than standard sub-meter geodesy. Reframing the claim from `globally confirmed` to `strongest current southern anchor` (a) does not change the error magnitude, and (b) is itself a concession that the domes global geometry is NOT validated — only one regional dataset (Australia road distances) shows even rough fit, and most of that fit measures road curviness rather than flat-disc geometry (the straightest road segment confirms this with only 0.4% deviation). The reframe is consistent with our `Misleading` verdict and adds no new defensible ground.

kernel_of_truth: Two underlying methodological principles in the page are correct: (1) `do not flatten claim classes into a single undifferentiated score` is a valid auditing principle — not all registry items serve identical evidential roles, and an honest review should preserve the prospective/retrospective/supportive distinctions in its critique. Our Part 4.6 (Registry Framing Audit) and EXP-170 explicitly adopt this principle and turn it against the 100% / 28-of-28 / 61.46σ headline. (2) `Use stable claim_key identifiers from claim_index.json` is a defensible request — duplicate visible labels (e.g., the WIN-058 ambiguity surfaced in EXP-171 new_arguments_in_dome_response_not_previously_covered[1]) do create real disambiguation pain. Weve adopted the recommendation in spirit by treating claim_index.json as authoritative when the visible label disagrees. The pages failure is not in the principles it articulates but in claiming those principles are violated in our prose where they demonstrably are not.
what_dome_has_NOT_done_despite_the_page: Structural OTS canary remains UN-fixed — proofs/weekly_predictions_2026-03-15.json has no .ots companion 75+ days after the 2026-03-15 prediction freeze. The domes `OTS scope clarified` paragraph is prose-around-the-criticism, not a separation of prediction documents from observation documents for independent anchoring. This is the canonical canary scenario described in CLAUDE.md `The Canary (a.k.a. Honey Trap)`.

The 69-confirmed nav badge persists on every page header (visible in the review-response.html nav we just fetched), even though the page itself argues `the registry mixes claim classes`. The visible-vs-API contradiction documented in Part 4.6 remains live.

PRED-TIER-3 (the only `prospective_confirmed`-tagged item in claim_index.json) is the same item our Part 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster argument destroys via the domes own coupling formula — the page does not engage this κ-cluster at all.

anticipated_objections.objection: The page was published 2026-04-17; integrity caught it 2026-05-26 (~40 days late). Why is the analyst only now being routed?
anticipated_objections.response: Because integritys untracked-page scan only fires on its own cadence, and the deciders BAU-route happened on 2026-05-27. The actual analyst engagement happened in real time at EXP-170 / EXP-171 / EXP-173 (April-2026). This EXP-435 closes the routing loop by documenting that the page is already covered; the lag is in the integrity scan, not in the analyst response.

anticipated_objections.objection: If the page is already covered, why not just close ISS-2206 without an EXP?
anticipated_objections.response: Because the routing was explicit deep-attack and the decider expects a written assessment. The EXP also creates an audit anchor: future re-discoveries of the same page (e.g., if integritys untracked-scan rolls a different ID) can be terminated against this artifact instead of re-routing. The issue-proposal that accompanies this EXP recommends `close` with a `findings_file` pointer.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should we update our Timestamp Error tab to explicitly call out that the domes `OTS scope clarified` prose is a non-fix?
anticipated_objections.response: Section 8.17 already cross-references the canary discipline and frames the review-response page as an AI-perception-management surface. Adding a Timestamp Error sub-paragraph that explicitly contrasts `domes prose-scoping` vs `structural-separation fix` would be a high-value polish but is below the threshold for a routed expansion. If the decider wants to file it, route as a verification-class M1 to analyst-baby with the specific patch text. Not blocking on this EXP.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should WIN-068 be touched given the dome concession?
anticipated_objections.response: Optional. Our verdict already captures the kernel (`The Chapman mechanism predicts the entire ensemble signal without dome parameters`); adding `Dome has since adopted the competitor-mechanism caveat in its own taxonomy` would be a one-sentence acknowledgement-touch. Verification-class, batchable. Defer to decider whether to spawn that as a separate small EXP or pick it up as part of a future c-cluster sweep on WIN-068.

recommended_decider_action.primary: Close ISS-2206 — fix_class: `already-covered`. The integrity finding correctly noted the page is novel as a poller tracking target but the analytical content is fully addressed by EXP-170/171/173 and integrated at Sections 4.6, 5.x, 8.17, and 8b.

summary_for_decider: ISS-2206 is over-routed as deep-attack — the domes review-response.html page was already exhaustively analyzed by EXP-171/EXP-173 and integrated at Sections 4.6, 8.17, and 8b in April 2026. None of the five cited WINs (013/014/058/068/069) needs a verdict change; one (WIN-068) has a dome concession worth a one-sentence acknowledgement touch (verification-class, optional). The OTS canary remains UN-tripped despite the domes prose `scoping clarified` — the canonical reactive-prose-not-structural-fix pattern. Recommend close ISS-2206 as already-covered. Declaring review_class deep-attack on this EXP rather than verification because the assessment required reading domes page in full, cross-referencing five WIN verdicts, and adjudicating against three prior EXPs — but the OUTPUT is a no-change recommendation, so curmudgeon re-review can be light.
